_comment: Distilled from PTCA, Systemic thrombolysis, CABG, IABP_Medco.pdf and PTCA, Systemic thrombolysis, CABG, IABP_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Diffuse retrosternal chest pain, heaviness or constriction radiating to arm or neck or back, with sweating with/without ST elevation in ECG
History of coronary artery disease (CAD)/ multiple risk factors for MI
Artery diameter > 3.5 mm & legion length < 18mm & non-diabetic (for Bare Metal Stents)
Beneficiaries needing non-cardiac surgery within 8 weeks of angioplasty (for Bare Metal Stents)
Left Main Complex Lesions (for Drug Eluting Stents)
Venous Graft (Post CABG) (for Drug Eluting Stents)
Chronic Total Occlusion (for Drug Eluting Stents)
Primary PCI In STEMI for Culprit Vessel (for Drug Eluting Stents)

clinical_thresholds.note: in one or more of the main coronary artery

preauth.label: Cardiac enzymes (Troponins T/ I (if not available then CPK-MB)
Routine Biochemistry (Haemogram, urea, creatinine, electrolytes, sugar, fasting lipids, Liver function test, Urinalysis)

claim.label: Still image of the patient undergoing the procedure with date & time
Angiogram stills & report showing stent & post stent flow
Cardiac enzymes (Troponins T/ I (if not available then CPK-MB) (Post Procedure)
Barcode of the stent(s) used (Bare metal/ Drug eluting stent)

ppd_preauth.q: Consent duly signed by Patient & treating Cardiologist- Yes
Stenosis of >/=70% in one or more of the main coronary artery - Yes

cpd_claim.q: Does the patient have indications (documentary evidence, physical examination) of PTCA/ PCI i.e. h/o Diffuse retrosternal chest pain, heaviness or constriction radiating to arm or neck or back, with sweating with/without ST elevation in ECG; +/- history of coronary artery disease (CAD)/ multiple risk factors for MI?
Do procedure notes detail the steps of PTCA/ PCI performed with Angiogram stills showing stent & post stent flow, barcode of stent (Bare-metal/ drug eluting) & outcomes of Procedure done?

Are indications for using Bare metal/ Drug eluting stent (DES) mentioned? For ref. these may include: Artery diameter > 3.5 mm & legion length < 18mm & non-diabetic; If beneficiaries needs non- cardiac surgery within 8 weeks of angioplasty; Diabetic beneficiaries; Small Arteries, Diameter < 3mm; Long Lesions; Left Main Complex Lesions; Venous Graft (Post CABG); Chronic Total Occlusion; In-stent restenosis; Primary PCI In STEMI for Culprit Vessel in STEMI

Are the clinical notes and discharge documents available to show monitoring of vitals (BP, Heart Rate), appropriate post-op care, medications- Gp2b/3a Antagonists, Unfractionated Heparin, Anti-platelets (unless contraindicated), anti-thrombotics (unless contraindicated), anti-ischemic, High Dose Statins, ACE inhibitors, Beta blocker & advise including for follow-up?

common_queries: Consent not signed by Patient and treating Cardiologist.
Stenosis of main coronary artery documented below 70% without justification.
Angiogram stills not showing stent and post stent flow.
Barcode of stent(s) not available or illegible.
Stent type not justified as per clinical indications provided.
Clinical notes/discharge summary not showing proper monitoring of vitals, post-op care or medications.
Coronary Angiography stills & reports (showing blockage) not uploaded at pre-auth.
Cardiac enzymes (Troponins T/ I or CPK-MB) not uploaded at pre-auth.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Cardiac catheterization lab, Cardiac Care Unit & qualified Cardiologist (for PTCA/ PCI/ IABP)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for PTCA only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms & investigation findings
Look for other causes of chest pain as well (ongoing or within 12 hours): Dissection of Aorta, Pleuritis/ pneumonitis/ embolism/ pneumothorax, pericardial rub, neuralgia or herpes
Refer the patient to angioplasty (PTCA/ PCI)/ thrombolysis capable hospital, if required
Prefer CABG over PTCA/PCI if patient is having diabetes mellitus with multi-vessel disease or left main disease
